NCT03954834.name: SURPASS-1
NCT03954834.group: Drug-naive T2D, tirzepatide 15 mg QW vs placebo (40 wk)

allOutcomes.title: HbA1c change at week 40, tirzepatide 5 mg vs placebo (%, MD; SURPASS-1)
HbA1c change at week 40, tirzepatide 10 mg vs placebo (%, MD; SURPASS-1)
HbA1c change at week 40, tirzepatide 15 mg vs placebo (%, MD; SURPASS-1)
Body-weight change at week 40, tirzepatide 5 mg vs placebo (kg, MD)
Body-weight change at week 40, tirzepatide 10 mg vs placebo (kg, MD)
Body-weight change at week 40, tirzepatide 15 mg vs placebo (kg, MD)

NCT03954834.snippet: Source: Rosenstock J et al. Lancet 2021;398:143-155 (SURPASS-1; multi-dose vs placebo).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 478 drug-naive T2D adults were randomized 1:1:1:1 to tirzepatide 5/10/15 mg QW or placebo for 40 weeks. HbA1c 7.0-9.5%, BMI >=23. Tirzepatide 15 mg arm n=121 analysed.

evidence.label: Primary — HbA1c Change at Week 40
evidence.text: HbA1c change from baseline at 40 weeks: tirzepatide 15 mg -2.07% vs placebo +0.04%; estimated treatment difference MD -2.11% (95% CI -2.41 to -1.82; P<0.0001). Every active dose met superiority.

evidence.label: Secondary — Body Weight and HbA1c <5.7%
evidence.text: Body weight change at 40 weeks: tirzepatide 15 mg -9.5 kg vs placebo -0.7 kg; ETD -8.8 kg (95% CI -10.6 to -7.0). 52% of 15 mg patients reached HbA1c <5.7% (normoglycaemia).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW — computer-generated central randomization. D2 LOW — double-blind placebo. D3 LOW — 93% completed. D4 LOW — central lab for HbA1c. D5 LOW — pre-specified SAP with mITT.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have been diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM).~* Are naïve to diabetes injectable therapies and have not used any oral antihyperglycemic medications (OAMs) during the 3 months preceding screening.~* Have HbA1c between ≥7.0% and ≤9.5%.~* Be of stable weight (± 5%) for at least 3 months before screening.~* Have a BMI ≥23 kilograms per meter squared (kg/m²) at screening.~Exclusion Criteria:~* Have type 1 diabetes mellitus.~* Have had chronic or acute pancreatitis a...

NCT03882970.name: SURPASS-3
NCT03882970.group: T2D on metformin, tirzepatide 15 mg QW vs insulin degludec titrated (52 wk, open-label)

allOutcomes.title: HbA1c change at week 52 (%, active-corrected MD, tirzepatide 15 mg)

NCT03882970.snippet: Source: Ludvik B et al. Lancet 2021;398:583-598 (SURPASS-3).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,437 insulin-naive T2D adults on stable metformin (with or without SGLT2i) randomized 1:1:1:1 open-label to tirzepatide 5/10/15 mg QW or insulin degludec titrated to fasting SMBG <5.0 mmol/L for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary — HbA1c at Week 52
evidence.text: HbA1c change from baseline at 52 weeks: tirzepatide 15 mg -2.37% vs insulin degludec -1.34%; estimated treatment difference MD -1.04% (95% CI -1.17 to -0.90; P<0.0001). All tirzepatide doses superior.

evidence.label: Body Weight and Hypoglycaemia
evidence.text: Weight: tirzepatide 15 mg -12.9 kg vs degludec +2.3 kg; ETD -15.2 kg. Hypoglycaemia incidence < degludec. No severe hypo on tirzepatide; 2% severe hypo on degludec.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW — central randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS — open-label design (active-comparator insulin vs subcutaneous tirzepatide). D3 LOW — 91% completion. D4 LOW — central HbA1c. D5 LOW — SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Participants must:~  * Have been diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM)~ * Have HbA1c between ≥7.0% and ≤10.5%~ * Be on stable treatment with unchanged dose of metformin or metformin plus an SGLT-2 inhibitor for at least 3 months before screening~ * Be of stable weight (± 5%) for at least 3 months before screening~ * Have a BMI ≥25 kilograms per meter squared (kg/m2) at screening~Exclusion Criteria:~* Participants must not:~  * Have type 1 diabetes mellitus~ * Have...

NCT04039503.name: SURPASS-5
NCT04039503.group: T2D on insulin glargine +/- metformin, tirzepatide 15 mg QW vs placebo as add-on (40 wk)

allOutcomes.title: HbA1c change at week 40 (%, placebo-corrected MD, tirzepatide 15 mg add-on)

NCT04039503.snippet: Source: Dahl D et al. JAMA 2022;327:534-545 (SURPASS-5).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 475 T2D adults on insulin glargine with or without metformin were randomized 1:1:1:1 double-blind to tirzepatide 5/10/15 mg QW or placebo as add-on to insulin glargine for 40 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary — HbA1c at Week 40
evidence.text: HbA1c change from baseline at 40 weeks: tirzepatide 15 mg -2.59% vs placebo -0.93%; estimated treatment difference MD -1.66% (95% CI -1.84 to -1.48; P<0.001). All tirzepatide doses superior.

evidence.label: Weight and Safety
evidence.text: Body weight change: tirzepatide 15 mg -10.9 kg vs placebo +1.7 kg; ETD -12.6 kg. GI adverse events (nausea) more common but largely mild-moderate; severe hypoglycaemia rare (<2%).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW — central randomization. D2 LOW — double-blind placebo with matched volume. D3 LOW — 95% completion. D4 LOW — central HbA1c. D5 LOW — pre-specified SAP.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have been diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and have been treated with insulin glargine (U100), once daily with or without metformin ≥3 months prior to screening visit.~* Have HbA1c between ≥7.0% and ≤10.5%.~* Have a stable weight (± 5%) for at least 3 months before screening.~* Have a body mass index (BMI) ≥23 kilograms per meter squared (kg/m²) at screening.~Exclusion Criteria:~* Have type 1 diabetes mellitus.~* Have had chronic or acute pancreatitis any tim...

NCT03730662.name: SURPASS-4
NCT03730662.group: T2D + high CV risk, tirzepatide 15 mg SC weekly vs insulin glargine (52-wk HbA1c MD; 3P-MACE adjudicated, CV signal)

allOutcomes.title: HbA1c change at week 52 tirzepatide 15 mg vs insulin glargine (MD; SURPASS-4 is the only hard-outcome SURPASS with adjudicated 3P-MACE HR 0.74, 0.51-1.08)

NCT03730662.snippet: Source: Del Prato S et al. Lancet 2021;398:1811-1824 (SURPASS-4). Only hard-CV-outcome SURPASS.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Endocrinology | Tirzepatide in Type 2 Diabetes (SURPASS) v1.0
